components.name: dinitrogen gas (N2)
components.evidence: resuspended in sterilized anoxic N- and Fe-free Burk's medium ... All groups were supplemented with 5 mM Fe(III)-citrate, degassed with N2 ... The N2 + Fe and Ar + Fe groups were supplemented with 5 mM Fe(III)-citrate and degassed with N2.

media.base_medium: Modified anoxic Burk's medium (N-free, Azotobacter-type minimal medium), fully specified inline in the paper; no external base-medium reference cited.

sources_chased.how: Recipe given verbatim inline in main text Materials & Methods; paper cites no external reference for Burk's medium composition.

sources_chased.what: Supplementary Materials Section S1 / Table S1
sources_chased.how: Section S1 describes only the FWAFC medium used for G. sulfurreducens PCA (a different, non-target medium); it does not add composition for Burk's medium.

media.notes: The paper defines the complete modified anoxic Burk's medium inline (per liter): 20 g mannitol, 0.2 g KH2PO4, 0.8 g K2HPO4, 0.2 g MgSO4·7H2O, 0.1 g CaSO4·2H2O, trace Na2MoO4·2H2O and FeCl3, pH 7.0; N-free (N2 headspace) for diazotrophic N fixation, supplemented with 5 mM Fe(III)-citrate as the electron acceptor for the target group. It is a defined Azotobacter-type minimal medium with no vitamins, no complex ingredients, no added reductant (cysteine/sulfide), and no trace-element stock beyond the trace Mo and Fe stated. All target current_components are accounted for; the current 'Fe2+' entry corresponds to a reduction product, not a supplied ingredient (supplied iron is Fe(III) from FeCl3 and Fe(III)-citrate). Recipe is complete as published; no external base reference exists to chase.
